query: South Africa IEC historical registration weekend results municipal elections new voter registrations numbers
answer:
## South Africa IEC Historical Registration Weekend Results — Municipal Elections

### Overview

The Independent Electoral Commission (IEC) of South Africa conducts voter registration weekends ahead of major elections (both general and municipal). These weekends are dedicated periods where citizens can register to vote, update their registration details, or re-register in a different voting district. Below is a comprehensive synthesis of the available historical data.

---

### 1. Voters' Roll Growth Over Time

The total registered voters' roll has grown steadily since 1999, though the rate of growth has been decelerating:

| Election Type | Year | Registered Voters | Period Growth |
|---|---|---|---|
| General | 1999 | 18,172,751 | — |
| Municipal | 2000 | ~18.5 million (est.) | — |
| General | 2004 | 20,674,926 | +13.8% |
| Municipal | 2006 | ~21–22 million (est.) | — |
| General | 2009 | 23,181,997 | +12.1% |
| Municipal | 2011 | 23,650,000 | — |
| General | 2014 | 25,388,082 | +9.5% |
| Municipal | 2016 | ~25,800,000 (est.) | — |
| General | 2019 | 26,756,649 | +5.4% |
| Municipal | 2021 | 26,204,579 | — |
| General | 2024 | 27,782,081 | +3.8% |
| Municipal | 2026 (upcoming) | 27,670,000 (as of Dec 2025) | — |

> **Note:** The 2021 municipal roll (26.2M) was slightly lower than the 2019 general election roll (26.8M), reflecting mortality attrition (~35,000/month) and reduced registration activity during COVID-19.

---

### 2. Registration Weekend — Specific New Voter Registration Numbers

#### 2019 General Election (Combined Two Weekends)
- **New registrations: 1,194,314**
- Re-registrations (different district): 1,964,174
- Re-registrations (same district): 2,081,171
- **Total registration interactions: ~5.2 million** across both weekends
- Source: PMG committee meeting 27861 (IEC CEO Sy Mamabolo briefing, 12 February 2019)

#### 2021 Municipal Election
- Only **one registration weekend** was held (18–19 September 2021) instead of the usual two, due to COVID-19 budget constraints
- The IEC's registration target was **not achieved**
- Online voter self-registration was introduced for the first time
- Final voters' roll: **26,204,579**
- Specific new registration numbers for this single weekend were not found in accessible sources

#### 2024 General Election — First Registration Weekend (18–19 November 2023)
- **New registrations: 568,374** (19.57% of total activity)
- **Total registration activity: 2,904,037**
- Re-registrations (same district): ~1,400,000
- Re-registrations (different district): 929,564
- Online portal transactions: 196,511
- **Young voters (aged 16–29): 445,089 = 78.31% of all new registrations**
- Net voters' roll increase: ~26.3M → ~26.8M (+~500,000)
- Source: Parliament of South Africa press release; PMG meeting 38023

#### 2026 Municipal Elections (Upcoming — Scheduled 4 November 2026)
- Registration weekends scheduled: **20–21 June 2026** and **August 2026**
- As of December 2025: **27.67 million** registered voters
- January–September 2025: **305,200 new voters** registered
- April 2025 update: **258,838 new voters** registered through various platforms (majority young people)
- The IEC is recruiting **70,000+ employees** for registration weekend operations
- Online voter registration push commenced 9 March 2026

---

### 3. Key Trends

- **Decelerating roll growth**: Growth has slowed from ~13.8% per 5-year cycle (1999–2004) to ~3.8% (2019–2024), reflecting demographic saturation.
- **Youth dominance in new registrations**: ~78% of new registrations in the November 2023 weekend were aged 16–29.
- **Mortality attrition**: The IEC loses approximately **35,000 voters per month** from the roll due to deaths.
- **Declining municipal turnout**: Voter turnout in municipal elections has been volatile and trending down — 48.4% (2000), 48.4% (2006), 57.6% (2011), 57.9% (2016), **45.8% (2021, historic low)**.
- **Digital transformation**: Online self-registration was introduced in 2021 and recorded 196,511 transactions during the November 2023 weekend alone.
- **Address completeness improvement**: Complete addresses on the voters' roll improved from 34% (2016) to 83% (2018) to 91% (2020/21).

---

### 4. Data Gaps

- **2000, 2006 municipal elections**: Exact registered voter figures and registration weekend-specific data are not available in accessible online sources; the IEC website was blocked by Cloudflare.
- **2011, 2016 municipal elections**: Specific registration weekend new voter numbers were not found in accessible parliamentary or media sources.
- **2021 municipal registration weekend**: While the weekend occurred on 18–19 September 2021, the specific count of new registrations that weekend was not located.

---

### Sources

- Wikipedia: Articles on 2000, 2006, 2011, 2016, 2021, 2026 SA municipal elections; 1999, 2004, 2009, 2014, 2019, 2024 SA general elections
- Parliament of South Africa: Press release on 2024 voter registration weekend results
- Parliamentary Monitoring Group (PMG): Committee meetings 27861 (Feb 2019), 32692 (2021 readiness), 33799 (IEC annual report), 38023 (Nov 2023 registration weekend)
- SABC News: Voter registration coverage

key_facts: The South Africa voters' roll has grown from 18.17 million (1999) to 27.78 million (2024), a ~53% increase over 25 years, but growth rate has decelerated from ~13.8% per cycle to ~3.8%.
For the 2019 general election, combined registration weekends produced 1,194,314 new registrations out of ~5.2 million total interactions.
For the 2024 general election first registration weekend (Nov 2023), 568,374 new voters registered out of 2,904,037 total registration activities.
78.31% of new registrations at the November 2023 weekend were young voters aged 16-29 (445,089 people).
The 2021 municipal election had only one registration weekend (18-19 Sept 2021) instead of the usual two, due to COVID-19 budget constraints; the IEC's registration target was not achieved.
The 2021 municipal election recorded the lowest voter turnout in SA democratic history at 45.79%.
The IEC loses approximately 35,000 voters per month from the roll due to mortality.
Online voter self-registration was first introduced for the 2021 municipal elections; by November 2023 weekend it recorded 196,511 online transactions.
For the upcoming 2026 municipal elections (scheduled 4 Nov 2026), registration weekends are set for 20-21 June and August 2026; as of December 2025, 27.67 million voters are registered.
Between January-September 2025, 305,200 new voters registered ahead of the 2026 municipal elections.
Complete address data on the voters' roll improved from 34% (2016) to 91% (2020/21).
The 2021 municipal election voters' roll stood at 26,204,579, slightly below the 2019 general election roll of 26,756,649, reflecting COVID-era registration challenges and mortality attrition.
Specific registration weekend new voter numbers for the 2000, 2006, 2011, and 2016 municipal elections were not found in accessible sources.

data_points.metric: New registrations - 2019 general election (both weekends combined)
New registrations - 2024 general election first weekend (Nov 2023)
Youth share of new registrations (16-29) - Nov 2023 weekend
Monthly voter attrition due to mortality
